candidate_problems.text: Identify a candidate representation of the zeta function in which moving zeros off the critical line would simultaneously break multiple established constraints, making RH nearly forced.

Determine whether analogies to finite-field cases or random-matrix statistics can be strengthened into genuinely load-bearing components of an RH explanation rather than remaining suggestive heuristics.
Develop a method for distinguishing promising explanatory cores in RH from programs that are merely hard to vary because they are technically rigid but explanatorily shallow.
